message:
feat: add structured shutdown budgets and semaphore-based collector backpressure

Wire asupersync's structured cancellation through the full stack so
that long-running commands (daemon, watch, web) drain cleanly on
SIGINT/SIGTERM within a configurable grace period (VC_SHUTDOWN_GRACE_SECS,
default 30s).

Key changes:

  vc_config -- Add max_concurrent_collectors and max_concurrent_per_machine
    fields to CollectorConfig with validation and lint rules.

  vc_collect/remote -- Replace the single max_concurrent buffer_unordered
    limit with a two-tier semaphore scheme: a global Semaphore caps total
    in-flight collector operations across the fleet, and per-machine
    DashMap<String, Semaphore> caps operations against any single host.
    Permits are acquired per-machine first (avoids starving other hosts),
    then globally, and are released automatically via RAII guards.  Six
    new tests verify serialisation, cancellation, permit leak-freedom,
    and large-workload capacity.

  vc_cli -- Introduce run_with_cx so main.rs can thread the root Cx into
    CLI dispatch.  Extract daemon, watch, and web command bodies into
    standalone async fns that accept a ShutdownReceiver.  Add
    run_with_shutdown_budget helper that races the command future against
    OS signals, then drains within the shutdown budget.

  vc_web -- Add run_with_shutdown accepting an arbitrary shutdown future
    so the CLI can plug its own ShutdownReceiver.  Upgrade the default
    shutdown_signal to listen for both SIGINT and SIGTERM via asupersync.

  main.rs -- Clone root_cx and pass it to cli.run_with_cx so shutdown
    cancellation propagates from the asupersync runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
